$comment: SEC-META-01 pending allowlist: every security-guide SEC id without a shipped test MUST map to its owning task file (security-guide §2.1.4-5). The owning task file MUST declare the id on its "**SEC ids owned by THIS task:**" marker line — a prose mention does not confer ownership, and a task naming an id only to disclaim it fails the gate. Entries are removed when the owning task ships a verbatim-id test title; task 28 requires this file empty. An id that is BOTH titled and listed here fails the gate: the row and the title cannot both be true. — SEC-AUTH-09 (task 28): LEG 2 shipped (no pushed op payload carries verifier material — packages/harness/test/security/sec-auth-09-payloads.test.ts, titled for invariant I-13, which it fully discharges). LEG 1 — 'verifiers exist only inside the SQLCipher DB', i.e. scan app storage for salt/verifier bytes — is UNPROVEN and cannot be proven in CI: it needs REAL SQLCipher, and CI has none (better-sqlite3 ships no SQLCipher; op-sqlite is a JSI module that cannot run in Node). It belongs to the emulator/device lane (task 27a), alongside SEC-DEV-06's L6 leg. Per security-guide §2.1.6 a title claims the WHOLE id, so no test titles SEC-AUTH-09 while leg 1 is unrun — this row stays until 27a runs it and reports back, and it is never asserted against a fake.
